probable_participant_cves.description: CVEs from Glasswing participant companies that meet probable self-scan criteria: participant vendor + HTTP-parsing-adjacent + no prior attacker exploitation + no obvious third-party researcher credit + bug pattern consistent with automated scanning (input validation, logic, cert handling). Separate from confirmed/attributed Glasswing count.

entries.notes: Batch of 3 ISE RCEs disclosed simultaneously — cluster pattern consistent with systematic AI audit
Same batch as -20180; identical attack pattern, different code path
Third ISE RCE in same batch; requires full admin creds vs read-only for -20180/-20186
Certificate validation flaw in SSO integration. Same disclosure batch as ISE bugs. Pattern consistent with TLS/cert-handling audit sweep

monthly_cve_2026_notes.2026-04: MTD through 2026-04-22 (22 days); extrapolated full-month ~5764 (4227 * 30/22). +342 net CVEs between day 21 and day 22 snapshots, driven primarily by NVD back-population of 2026-04-21 (finalized at 359 entries vs the 35 we saw 24h earlier) — classic 24h catch-up. Day 22 itself running at 18 entries same-day. Daily rate ~192/day. Still tracking below March (6304) and below the +500/mo conservative Glasswing anchor, but the gap has narrowed.

rhel8_security.note: stack.watch dropped the /8.0 version-specific path; use parent URL (covers all RHEL versions). 2026 YTD: 99 per stack.watch as of 2026-04-18. Red Hat security data API also 404s — stack.watch is the best available source.

kev_monthly_2026_notes.2026-04: 22 entries added 2026-04-01..2026-04-20 (per CISA KEV catalog version 2026.04.21 released 2026-04-21T17:10Z). No new KEV additions between 2026-04-20 and 2026-04-22 — catalog refreshed but content unchanged. April total holding at 22 (ahead of Q1 monthly average ~24). Notable 2026-04-20 batch: Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN Manager cluster (CVE-2026-20122, -20128, -20133) — Cisco (Glasswing participant), HTTP-adjacent management-plane bugs; plus CVE-2025-2749 (Kentico Xperience), CVE-2023-27351 (PaperCut NG/MF), CVE-2025-48700 (Zimbra), CVE-2025-32975 (Quest KACE SMA), CVE-2024-27199 (JetBrains TeamCity). Earlier-April entries unchanged: CVE-2026-34197 (Apache ActiveMQ 2026-04-16), CVE-2026-32201 (SharePoint 0-day 2026-04-14), CVE-2026-21643 (Fortinet FortiClient EMS SQLi 2026-04-13), CVE-2026-34621 (Adobe Acrobat prototype pollution 2026-04-13), CVE-2026-1340 (Ivanti EPMM 2026-04-08), CVE-2026-35616 (Fortinet FortiClient EMS 2026-04-06), CVE-2026-3502 (TrueConf Client 2026-04-02), CVE-2026-5281 (Google Dawn 2026-04-01); plus 5 legacy re-adds on 2026-04-13/14 (CVE-2009-0238, CVE-2012-1854, CVE-2020-9715, CVE-2023-21529, CVE-2023-36424) and CVE-2025-60710 Microsoft Windows.

exploit_watch_list.description: HTTP-parsing-adjacent CVEs predicted to be actively exploited. Analyst adds entries; refresh agent checks KEV status.
exploit_watch_list.focus: HTTP-parsing attack surface only: web servers, template engines, TLS terminators, reverse proxy components, API frameworks, browser-delivered payloads

server.product: Cisco ISE (crafted HTTP → RCE, read-only admin)

exploit_intelligence.description: Tracks public exploit availability for watch list CVEs across ExploitDB, Metasploit, and GitHub PoC repos. Updated daily by refresh agent.
